• Our Long-term View - The last few years have been characterized by increased

global trade and have a meaningful impact on effective vessel supply. Combined
with the potential for a multi-year cyclical rebound in China's economic activity

volatility on top of a secular tightness driven by stable bulk commodity demand and
a slower fleet growth owing to a relatively low orderbook.

Happy New Year!
Following the start of the year, we see continuous activity on both dry and tanker markets.
The dry cargo market has held up with a steady flow of transactions, while the tanker sector has seen particularly strong momentum, driven by significant VLCC deals that have set a confident tone for the opening weeks of the year.
Dry Cargo
In the larger segment, we are hearing rumors of a 2011 Japanese built Capesize being committed at levels of USD 32.5 m net, while at the Kamsarmax front, the CENTURY SHANGHAI (ABT 81K DWT, 2018, CHENGXI) has been sold via auction for USD 25.02 m.
In the Ultramax segment, the STARRY NIGHT (ABT 61K DWT, 2022, NANTONG COSCO) achieved a price of USD 32.5 m, basis March - April delivery.
Furthermore, another auction sale has been concluded, with the Supramax JIANG YUAN NAN JING (ABT 50K DWT, 2003, NACKS) achieving levels of USD 7.54 m.
In the Handysize sector, the FETHIYE-M (ABT 37K DWT, 2011, SPP GOSEONG) has now been sold to Greek interests in the high USD 12 m, while the BULKER BEE 30 (ABT 34K DWT, 2010, TK) has been sold at USD 11.3 m. Finally, we are hearing of a 2016 Tsuneishi Cebu built Handysize being committed for levels of USD 19.1 m.
Tanker
The key transaction of the week involved c/o Frontline selling 8 x VLCCs (2015-2016 built - 1st generation ECO m/e) for an aggregate value of USD 831.5 m, with deliveries scheduled during Q1 2026. In parallel, the owners have agreed to acquire 9 x Newbuilding VLCCs with scrubbers for the amount of USD 1.224 bn, with deliveries running from Q3 2026 throughout Q2 2027.
In addition, c/o CMB.TECH have sold 6 x VLCCs (2007-2016 built) enbloc, with deliveries also scheduled for Q1 202

After the significant S&P activity observed last week, especially driven by the VLCC segment,the wet market remains highly active, with substantial movement observed across all segments.
On the dry side, although there has been some softening on rates, S&P demand remains present and acquisitions continue to emerge, with buyers adopting a cautious but opportunistic approach.
Dry Cargo
This week, dry bulk activity was concentrated in the larger segments, where c/o Berge Bulk sold their scrubber-fitted Newcastlemax BERGE MOLDOVEANU (ABT 211K DWT, 2020, BOHAI, SCRUBBER), for a price in the region of USD 74 m to Korean interests.
Furthermore, we are hearing of two Capesize units being sold this week, the FRONTIER KOTOBUKI (174K DWT, 2011, NAMURA) which was inviting offers yesterday, we understand that has been committed for USD 31 m, and another vintage unit which is built in Japan 2005 at a price of USD 20.5 m.
In the geared segment, the Ultramax OCEAN JASMIN (ABT 63K DWT, 2019, COSCO HI ZHOUSHAN) has been sold in the region of USD 28.5 m basis TC attached until max June 2026.
Unconfirmed rumors suggest two Ultramax transactions being concluded, including a 2012 Japanese built unit at a price in the low USD 19 m and a 2020 Chinese built vessel, said to be sold for xs USD 30 m.
On the smaller segment, c/o Straits Orient Lines sold their Handysize TBC PRAISE (ABT 36K DWT, 2012, HYUNDAI MIPO) for USD 14.4 m, while on the Chinese built front, we understand that a 37K DWT, 2012 unit is being negotiated in the levels of low/mid USD 11 m.
Tanker
The tanker sector has remained busy, VLCC transactions continued to unfold, while demand remains notably strong throughout different sizes, reflecting sustained buyer interest and confident sentiment.
On the LR segment, c/o Scorpio Tankers have sold their scrubber-fitted LR2 STI KINGSWAY (ABT 109K DWT, 2015, SUNGDONG, SCRUBBER) at USD 57.5 m to undisclosed

the levels of low/mid USD 11 m.

The tanker sector has remained busy, VLCC transactions continued to unfold, while demand remains notably strong throughout different sizes, reflecting sustained buyer interest and confident sentiment.
On the LR segment, c/o Scorpio Tankers have sold their scrubber-fitted LR2 STI KINGSWAY (ABT 109K DWT, 2015, SUNGDONG, SCRUBBER) at USD 57.5 m to undisclosed buyers.

Another active week on the tanker front, with a steady flow of transactions and ongoing negotiationstaking place across all vessel segments.
On the dry side, interest remains high with several new sales emerging as market sentiment strengthens.
Dry Cargo
On the Kamsarmax front, the MIAO XIANG (ABT 81K DWT, 2013, JIANGSU EASTERN) has been sold at USD 16.8 m, while on the Panamax segment, the KT BIRDIE (ABT 74K DWT, 2011, SASEBO HI) changed hands at USD 16.5 m to Greek interests.
We are also hearing that a 2020 Chinese built Ultramax has been sold for USD 30.1 m. Meanwhile, THE LOVING (ABT 58K DWT, 2007, TSUNEISHI CEBU) has been committed at USD 11.7 m.
On the Handysize segment, we are seeing several transactions unfolding and negotiations taking place, highlighting also buyers' appetite for smaller tonnage. The YANGTZE GRACE & YANGTZE HAPPINESS (ABT 32K DWT, 2012, JIANGMEN NANYANG) were sold for USD 9.8 m each to separate Chinese interests. According to market rumors a 37K DWT 2008 Japanese built Handysize has been sold for USD 11.4 m, whilst a 2015 Japanese built 36K DWT Handysize is in firm discussions in the high USD 18's m.
Tanker
On the Suezmax segment, c/o Okeanis have agreed to acquire two resale Suezmaxes currently under construction at Daehan Shipbuilding at a price of USD 99.3 m per vessel with delivery within the second quarter of 2026.
Meanwhile, we are hearing a 2009 Chinese built Suezmax has been sold at USD 42.5 m. Lastly, two modern LR2s 109K DWT 2024 built are rumored sold at USD 84.5 m each.

Compared to previous weeks, the dry bulk market has shown increased strength.The gearless tonnage led the way with few mid aged KMXs and PMXs being sold.
On the wet side, following an exceptionally strong start of the year, sales activity moderated. However, overall sentiment remains constructive, supported by continued interest especially in the crude sector.
Dry Cargo
On the gearless segment a couple of Kamsarmaxes have been sold. The RIZE (ABT 81K DWT, 2012, HYUNDAI MIPO) at circa USD 17.5m, and a 20-year-old Japanese built ship that, according to rumors, has achieved levels in the high USD 10'sm.
On the Panamax front, the GLOBAL BONANZA (ABT 75K DWT, 2011, SASEBO) sold at USD 15.3 m to Greek interests, basis surveys due. We are hearing that a 2006 LME has also been sold for USD 9.7m.
Meanwhile, the Ultramax MITSOS (ABT 63K DWT, 2013, CHENGXI) was acquired by Greek interests for USD 20.5m.

Following the numerous VLCCs sales of the past few weeks, another vintage ship has been reported sold, namely the DHT BAUHINIA (ABT 301K DWT, 2007, DAEWOO) that fetched a price of USD 51.5m basis June-July delivery.

Snp markets have maintained strong sentiment across sectors, building on a positive start to the year.
On the dry bulk side, the prevailing trend continues to favor larger tonnage transactions, supported by firm freight rates despite the approaching Lunar New Year, a period that is traditionally slower for market activity.
Meanwhile, the tanker market has recorded another strong week, with demand continuing to build rapidly and buyer appetite remaining robust. Numerous discussions are currently underway, and we are expecting to be able to report more sales in the coming weeks.
Dry Cargo
On the bulk carrier front, several transactions occurred across a wide range of sizes.
At the larger end, the Capesize ROBUSTO (ABT 174 DWT, 2006, SWS, SCRUBBER) was sold to Chinese interests for USD 19.5 m with delivery in Far East between April - May 2026.
In the Post Panamax segment, the JP CARETTA (ABT 88K DWT, 2008, IMABARI, SCRUBBER) was reported sold at region USD 13 m, while the Kamsarmax GASTONE (ABT 81K DWT, 2012, SUNGDONG) achieved a price of USD 17.6 m.
Within the geared segment, the Supramax SPAR CANIS (ABT 53K DWT, 2006, SHANGHAI CHENGXI) was reported sold at region USD 9 m to Chinese buyers, and the Handysize JETSTREAM (ABT 34K, 2012, SPP GOSEONG) changed hands at USD 13.5 m.
Tanker
On the tanker sector, the LR1 ATHIRI (ABT 74K DWT, 2010, SPP SACHEON) was acquired by Greek interests at USD 25 m.

Tankers and Bulkers continue to benefit from overall firm market sentiment, supporting steady levels of secondhand activity across the board. Buyers remain engaged, with transactions materializing in both larger and mid-sized segments as confidence in earnings and asset values persists.
On the dry side, Chinese interests have been particularly active in the Capesize segment. Meanwhile, the tanker market maintains solid momentum across all sectors, with strong buying appetite from Far Eastern and Greek buyers.
Dry Cargo
On the Capesize front, activity has remained firm, with several notable transactions concluded during the week. The scrubber-fitted STAR SCARLETT (ABT 175K DWT, 2014, JINHAI, SCRUBBER) was acquired by Chinese interests at around USD 36 m. Meantime, the EPIC (ABT 182K DWT, 2010, ODENSE LINDO) was sold at USD 32.5 m, and the same aged Chinese built CAPE BRAZIL (ABT 177K DWT, 2010, SWS) achieved a price of USD 31 m.
In the Handysize segment, activity was more selective, yet present. The CS CANDY (ABT 37K DWT, 2012, TIANJIN XIANGANG, ICE CLASS 1C) was sold at levels in the mid to high USD 11s m.
Tanker
On tankers, the VLCC sector witnessed another landmark transaction, as c/o Pan Ocean secured 10 scrubber-fitted VLCCs from SK Shipping, including units built between 2008 and 2013. The enbloc deal concluded at around USD 668 m and includes attached time-charter agreements back to SK Shipping.
C/o Euronav have sold the sister VLCCs ILMA & INGRID (ABT 318K DWT, 2012, HYUNDAI HI) to S. Korean interests reportedly generating a combined capital gain of USD 98.2 m. The transaction highlights the strong demand and continued liquidity in the VLCC space, with a stronger preference towards Korean-built tonnage.
Meanwhile on Suezmaxes, c/o Nordic Tankers sold the NORDIC POLLUX (ABT 150K DWT, 2003, NKK (TSU)) at USD 25 m.
The product tanker segment has also been experiencing increased activity. On MRs

. Korean interests reportedly generating a combined capital gain of USD 98.2 m. The transaction highlights the strong demand and continued liquidity in the VLCC space, with a stronger preference towards Korean-built tonnage.
Meanwhile on Suezmaxes, c/o Nordic Tankers sold the NORDIC POLLUX (ABT 150K DWT, 2003, NKK (TSU)) at USD 25 m.
The product tanker segment has also been experiencing increased activity. On MRs, the UOG OSLO (ABT 46K DWT, 2010, HYUNDAI MIPO) was sold at USD 23.5 m, and the sisters ELANDRA FJORD & ELANDRA BALTIC (ABT 51K DWT, 2011, HYUNDAI MIPO) achieved USD 24.5 m each from Greek interests.

Activity has moderated this week as the Lunar New Year period temporarily slows market momentum.
But despite seasonal slowdown, overall asset values continue to hold at firm levels across sectors, reflecting underlying confidence in earnings and sustained buying appetite.
With several offer deadlines scheduled for next week, activity is expected to accelerate, and we will be able to report further as the market momentum builds.
Dry Cargo
Further activity was recorded in the larger sizes, with the Capesize MICHALIS H (ABT 180K DWT, 2012, DAILAN, SCRUBBER) reported sold at USD 35.2 m, reflecting continued appetite for Capesize tonnage.
In the geared segment, the Supramax LIANSON DYNAMIC (ABT 53K DWT, 2006, CHENGXI) was reported sold at levels in xs USD 9 m.
Whilst we are hearing of a 2010 Japanese built Supramax being committed in levels close to USD 15 m.
Tanker
On the VLCC front, the scrubber fitted SINGAPORE SPIRIT (ABT 318K DWT, 2013, SWS) is reported sold at USD 84.5 m to C/O Sinokor.
Additionally, on the Aframax segment, the Korean built P. SOPHIA (ABT 105K DWT, 2009, Hyundai HI Ulsan) has changed hands achieving USD 36.65 m.
Meanwhile, in the MR space, the zinc coated CABO NEGRO II (ABT 47K DWT, 2006, SK Onishi) is reported sold at levels in the low USD 14 m range.

Both dry bulk and tanker secondhand markets have remained active, with bulker deals particularly active.
Transactions continue to materialize, reflecting sustained buyers' appetite despite asset prices across both sectors continuing to push at higher levels, supporting overall market confidence.
Dry Cargo
On the Kamsarmax front, c/o Agricore Shipping have concluded the sale of the ASL MOON (ABT 81K DWT, 2008, MITSUI TAMANO) at USD 13.6 m to Chinese interests.
Meanwhile, after having invited offers earlier in the week, the THREE SASKIAS (ABT 80K DWT, 2014, JMU TSU) has been committed at levels of USD 26.7 m to Far Eastern interests.
In addition, the GIEWONT (ABT 79K DWT, 2010, NEW CENTURY) has been concluded at USD 11.8 m, with Chinese being rumored as the buyers.
In the Supramax segment, c/o Densay Shipping have sold the SSI ERDOGAN BEY (ABT 50K DWT, 2010, OSHIMA) at USD 15 m, basis surveys freshly passed.
Finally, in the Handysize sector, the ADVENTURE (ABT 33K DWT, 2011, WEIHAI SAMJIN) has changed hands for USD 9.5 m basis a very prompt delivery with surveys imminently due.
Tanker
On the wet side, prices have continued to strengthen, with the present week forming no exception.
The scrubber fitted Suezmax SIGRUN (ABT 156K DWT, 2013, SUMITOMO) is reported sold at USD 65 m to C/O Naftomar.
Additionally, In the MR space, the scrubber-fitted sister vessels NORD MAVERICK and NORD MARVEL (ABT 50K DWT, 2020, HMD) have been committed on enbloc basis at levels in excess of USD 90 m to undisclosed buyers.

• Ample Iron Ore in China Amid Weakening Steel Sector Demand - Iron ore Breakwave Advisors Research
research@breakwaveadvisors.com
stockpiles in China have now reached a record 162 million tonnes, eclipsing the
previous peak established in 2018. Despite this surplus and generally sluggish steel
demand, prices remain resilient, hovering just below the $100/mt mark, stabilized
by the centralized procurement strategies of the China Mineral Resources Group
(CMRG). Much like its strategic management and increase of crude oil reserves,
China's continued aggressive acquisition of iron ore despite high inventories
remains an enigma; however, this activity has so far provided a significant tailwind
for the dry bulk shipping sector. While current iron ore prices may seem excessive
relative to the cooling domestic steel market, CMRG's oversight provides a
stabilizing mechanism capable of maintaining price equilibrium for both domestic
mills and global mining operators.
• Our Long-term View - The last few years have been characterized by increased
geopolitical uncertainty. Going forward, we expect such events to continue to affect
global trade and have a meaningful impact on effective vessel supply. Combined
with the potential for a multi-year cyclical rebound in China's economic activity
following the recent economic turmoil, dry bulk shipping should experience higher
volatility on top of a secular tightness driven by stable bulk commodity demand and
rather steady fleet growth.

Image reference: 2026-03-08_hd-hyundai-samho-snags-179-million-oil-tanker-order-in-greec_img1.jpg
HD Hyundai Samho said Friday it has secured a 263.2 billion won ($179 milion) order to build two oil tankers for a shipping company in Greece.
The deal covers two 157,000-deadweight-ton tankers, with deliveries scheduled by May 2029, the company said in a regulatory filing. The name of the Greece-based client was not disclosed.
HD Hyundai Samho is one of three shipbuilding affiliates under HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering (HD KSOE), along with HD Hyundai Heavy Industries and HD Hyundai Mipo Dockyard.
HD KSOE is a sub-holding company of HD Hyundai, Korea's leading shipbuilding group.
HD KSOE aims to secure $23.31 billion worth of orders this year, up from $18.16 billion last year. Source: Korea JoongAng Daily

Another strong week in the S&P market, particularly within the dry bulk segment, where fresh benchmark sales emerged at firmer levels. A couple of flagship deals in both the Kamsarmax and Handysize sectors set the tone, highlighting the upward momentum.
Demand remains strong across the board, while the limited availability of quality tonnage continues to push asset values.
Dry Cargo
The CCS ORCHID (ABT 82K DWT, 2017, JIANGSU NEW YANGZIJIANG) has been sold via online auction, at USD 27.24 m to Greek buyers. 18 buyers bid in total, with the reserve price being USD 22.78 m.
On the Handysize front, the modern SIDER IMABARI (ABT 40K DWT, 2024, SHIMANAMI) was sold for USD 34.8 m. Meanwhile, the Japanese controlled PACIFIC HARMONY (ABT 38K DWT, 2013, IMABARI), after being inspected by more than 15 Buyers last week, eventually sold for region USD 18 m basis drydock due in May 2026. For comparison, the last sistership sold was the TBC PRIME (ABT 38K DWT, 2011 MINAMINIPPON) that fetched USD 14.1 m back in November 2025.
Tanker
Two MR sales have been recorded, with the scrubber fitted sister vessels FINNANGER (ABT 46K DWT, 2009, HYUNDAI MIPO) and FJELLANGER (ABT 46K DWT, 2010, HYUNDAI MIPO) committed enbloc to c/o Msea Tankers, achieving a combined price of mid USD 47 m.

currently exhibits an interesting anomaly, characterized by a paradox of seaborne

market faces logistical pressures from the ongoing US-Iran conflict, port stockpiles
have surged to all-time highs as Chinese imports maintain a record pace. Despite
stagnant Chinese steel demand, prices have stabilized in the $100-$110 range,

and dry bulk markets seeing sustained support from the persistent inventory build-
up.

global trade and have a meaningful impact on effective vessel supply. Combined
with the potential for a multi-year cyclical rebound in China's economic activity
following the recent economic turmoil, dry bulk shipping should experience higher
volatility on top of a secular tightness driven by stable bulk commodity demand and
rather steady fleet growth.

The S&P market remains active, with asset values in both the tanker and dry bulk sectors remaining firm.
Modern eco-design vessels are drawing particularly strong interest and achieving premium levels, while older, non-eco tonnage is also maintaining liquidity, successfully attracting buyers and securing solid prices.
Dry Cargo
In the Kamsarmax segment, after inviting offers on Monday, the MG MERCURY (ABT 85K DWT, 2016, IMABARI, BEAM 35 M) was acquired by European interests at levels in the low USD 28's m.
On the Panamax front, the BARWON (ABT 78K DWT, 2015, SASEBO, ECO ME) invited offers earlier this week, attracting interest from up to 5 buyers, and has now been reported sold for xs USD 26 m.
On the geared segment, we understand that a 2021 Japanese Ultramax was sold for region USD 37 m, whilst the Supramax FIGEAC (ABT 53K DWT, 2011, CHENGXI) was sold for a price close to USD 12 m, basis surveys promptly due. Meanwhile, the older SOMNATH (ABT 55K DWT, 2005, OSHIMA) was sold at region USD 9 m.
Tankers
The Handy MR CHRYSOPIGI (ABT 38K DWT, 2006, GUANGZHOU) was sold at USD 9 m, basis surveys promptly due.

This week, the S&P market has adopted a more cautious tone, however, a fair number of transactions have still materialized across segments. Activity appears more selective and targeted, with the market continuing to be shaped by firm price expectations from Sellers.
Dry Cargo
In the Capesize sector, Japanese owners invited offers last week for the FRONTIER GARLAND (ABT 181K DWT, 2011, IMABARI) and is now reported sold to Greek interests at USD 36.4m.
Elsewhere, the modern Kamsarmax GIA INSPIRATION (ABT 85K DWT, 2022, CSSC TIANJIN) has changed hands at around USD 33.5m, while the Panamax AGRI KINSALE (ABT 77K DWT, 2009, OSHIMA SHIPBUILDING), controlled by c/o Fortune Ocean Shipping, was sold to undisclosed buyers at levels in the low USD 15m's.
In the Ultramax segment, the JIN RUI (ABT, 63K DWT, 2014, JIANGSU HANTONG) has been sold to Chinese interests at USD 24m.
Tankers
In the Tanker space, several vessels attracted offers this week. Among them were the Korean-owned 2 x VLCCs, C. PROGRESS (ABT 313K DWT, 2012, HD HYUNDAI) and C. INNOVATOR (ABT 297K DWT, 2012, DALIAN), as well as the MR Tanker, PRO ONYX (ABT 50K DWT, 2019, HYUNDAI MIPO), which are currently under negotiations.
Additionally, in the MR segment, another sale has been concluded, this time from c/o d'Amico Tankers, who have sold their HIGH SEAS (ABT 51K DWT 2012, HYUNDAI MIPO), for levels of high USD 27 m to Greek buyers.

This week in the S&P market, Owners appeared to be adopting a more cautious, wait-and-see approach towards engaging in new deals. At the same time prices continued following the upward trend of the past months and we saw numerous transactions concluding - particularly stemming from negotiations initiated in previous weeks.
Notably, attention has remained drawn to the Dry Bulk sector: Greeks are showing continued interest in ~15-year-old Kamsarmax tonnage, confirming a healthy demand in this segment. At the same time, Asian buyers demonstrated appetite for well-priced Supramaxes.
Dry Cargo
In the Supramax sector, the PETRUS (ABT 57K DWT, 2011, STX JINGHAE) was sold for a price in the high USD 13m's, and the MERCURY OCEAN (ABT 53K DWT, 2008, IWAGI ZOSEN) achieved a price of low USD 13m from Chinese buyers - both vessels surveys due in April 26'. We are hearing as well of a 2009 55K DWT Hyundai Vinashin built Supramax being sold for USD 13.4m.
In the Handysize front, the DL OLIVE (ABT 35K DWT, 2013, SPP) was acquired by Greek interests for USD 15.3m and the ATLANTIC SPIRIT (ABT 35K DWT, 2013, NANJING) achieved a price in the mid-high 12m's. On the smaller side, the SAFI FORTUNE (ABT 28K DWT, 2009, IMABARI) sold at around USD 9.7m.
Tanker
In the wet segment, activity was particularly focused on product tankers. The Chinese-built LR1 sister vessels, PM IMPERIAL and PM MONARCH (ABT 76K DWT, 2007, DALIAN) were reported sold enbloc for xs USD 20m each.
In the MR space, the Korean-built eco-vessels of Scorpio, STI BROOKLYN and STI BLACK HAWK (ABT 50K DWT, 2015, SPP / HMD, SCRUBBER), were concluded at around USD 35m each, both fitted with scrubbers and expected for delivery in Q2.

Desk Talk
Confirming the cautious sentiment of the previous weeks, this week's S&P market indicated a selective
and targeted appetite, with few but firmly priced transactions taking place in both the Dry and Wet
segments. While the general momentum feels steady, Buyers appear disciplined on pricing.
Notably, in the larger tanker space, well-positioned units are attracting firm interest, while in the dry
market, we notice an increased appetite for 15-20 year-old Japanese tonnage ranging from
Handysize to Kamsarmax, coming especially from the Far Eastern markets.
Dry Cargo
In the Newcastlemax sector, an enbloc transaction saw the sister vessels RTM CARTIER and RTM ZHENG
HE (ABT 205K DWT, 2012, HHIC PHILIPPINES) sold to Chinese buyers at USD 90m. Additionally, in the
Supramax space, the SUNNY ROYAL (ABT 58K DWT, 2011, KAWASAKI) was acquired for USD 18.5m by
Greek interests, while the PHOENIX K (ABT 54K DWT, 2007, OSHIMA) was sold for USD 14m to Chinese
interests. On the smaller sizes, the Handysize AEOLOS (ABT 31K DWT, 2001, SAIKI) sold for close to USD 7m,
while the more modern ASTRO ORION (ABT 37K DWT, 2017, AVIC WEIHAI) fetched a price of USD 21m.
Tanker
In the tanker market, activity was highlighted by the sale of the VLCC KASAGISAN (ABT 302K DWT, 2006,
MITSUI), reported sold at circa USD 60m, while the scrubber fitted Aframax P. ALIKI (ABT 105K DWT, 2010,
HYUNDAI HI) was committed to C/O Trafigura at USD 42.65m
